characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The Head of Surveillance and Security at the Montecito, Ed is a no-nonsense ex-CIAagent who has the respect (and fear) of most of the old guard in Vegas, as well as virtually all of the new establishment. He is soon promoted to President of Operations, and becomes responsible for the day-to-day running of the casino. Benevolent Boss: He truly cares about his employees and is often watching out for them. Dark and Troubled Past: He's done many things back in CIA days that he isn't proud of. A Father to His Men: Being the oldest character in the cast, he acts like a father figure to all of his employees, but most especially Danny. Field Promotion: Ed goes from chief of security to casino president on behalf of a mysterious owner who just bought a controlling interest in the Montecito.

characters.name: Ed Deline
characters.description: The Head of Surveillance and Security at the Montecito, Ed is a no-nonsense ex-CIAagent who has the respect (and fear) of most of the old guard in Vegas, as well as virtually all of the new establishment. He is soon promoted to President of Operations, and becomes responsible for the day-to-day running of the casino. Benevolent Boss: He truly cares about his employees and is often watching out for them. Dark and Troubled Past: He's done many things back in CIA days that he isn't proud of. A Father to His Men: Being the oldest character in the cast, he acts like a father figure to all of his employees, but most especially Danny. Field Promotion: Ed goes from chief of security to casino president on behalf of a mysterious owner who just bought a controlling interest in the Montecito.

characters.name: DannyMcCoy
characters.description: The central character of the series, Danny is a former Marine and childhood friend of Mary (unlike the rest of the cast, both of them are locals), who works at the Montecito as a member of the Surveillance and Security team. When Ed becomes President of Operations, Danny is promoted to Ed's former job of Security head. The show starts off with him giving voiceover explanations about what was happening, but this was quickly dropped part of the way into the first season. In the fourth season, Danny and Delinda get together and Delinda gets pregnant, and in the fifth season, he is promoted to Ed's old job as President of Operations. Actor Allusion: Josh Duhamel gets two alluding to his relationship (later marriage) withFergie, a member of theBlack Eyed Peas. When the band guest stars in the show, she refers to Danny as being hot when she sees him. In a later episode, Danny sees a poster of her hanging in a teenager's room and comments on it. Amateur Sleuth: He often acts much more like a detective than just normal security personnel. Bash Brothers: With Mike, even before Mike became part of the security team, they'd team up to take down theMonster of the Week. It happened so often Mike just eventually joined the security team. Battle Butler: While he's technically just security, he often gets odd jobs around that have him acting like an attendant, servant, valet, or waiter.

characters.name: Mary Connell
characters.description: The Montecito's Special Events Director, who is responsible for planning and organizing conventions, weddings, and any other events taking place at the hotel. Abusive Parents: Her father abused and molested her when she was young. Betty and Veronica: The Betty to the Mary x Danny x DelindaLove Triangle, as she's the kind and sensitiveChildhood Friendin comparison to Delinda's cool and seductive temptress persona. Beware the Nice Ones: Mary who is the most genuinely kind and sweet of the group has a fiery temper, is known to hold grudges,and murdered her abusive father to protect her sisters. Big Sister Instinct: Mary, who was sexually abused by her father when she was a kid, learns she has little half-sisters when her father attempts to "make amends". After learning this, she tearfully contacts her stepmother and informs her of what her father had done to her when she was her sisters age.After meeting her sisters, and immediately falling in love with them, when she learns from her stepmother that her father has begun abusing them too, she agrees to testify on their behalf. Ultimately after the trial goes lopsided, she outright murders him to protect them.

characters.name: Sam Marquez
characters.description: The Montecito's Casino Host, responsible for drawing"whales"(rich clients who gamble large amounts of money) to the casino and doing everything she can to keep them happy and betting. Brainy Brunette: She's a brunette and is extremely intelligent and possibly the most consistently competent person at the casino. The Charmer: She can be a charming sweet-talker when she wants to, which is partly why she's a good hostess. Her real personality is much more blunt and snarky. Corrupt Corporate Executive: She's a ruthless businesswoman whose sole interest is to get "whales" (high rollers and big spenders) to play at the Montecito for as long as possible.Sam:All's fair in love, war, and casino hosting. Crossover Couple: She has an on-and-off relationship with Detective Woody Hoyt fromCrossing Jordan.

characters.name: Mike Cannon
characters.description: The Montecito's Head Valet, Mike is actually a genius graduate of MIT who moved to Vegas for the adventure. After Danny is promoted to Head of Security, he brings Mike upstairs as a member of the Security team. In the fifth season, the recently promoted Danny makes him head of Surveillance and Security. Accidental Marriage:Mike and Piper got so drunk in one episode, they ended up getting married in one of Vegas' chapels. After some talking, they decide to stay married. Almighty Janitor: Mike is an engineer, MIT graduate, and nerd, yet he starts the series as a valet. He's called in to help the actual security staff several times during the first season. Despite his initial reluctance — he actually likes being a valet, and doesn't want the potential pay cut — he eventually becomes full-time security staff. In the final season, he's actually promoted to head of security. Bash Brothers: With Danny, even before he became part of the security team, they'd team up to take down theMonster of the Week. It happened so often he just eventually joined the security team. Beta Couple: He tends to have romantic subplots with female characters that don't last long, as theyPut on a Bus. It happened with Nessa in season 2, Sarasvati in season 3,Ship Teasewith Shannon in season 4, and anAccidental Marriageto Piper in season 5. Although he did at least end season 5 still together with Piper.

characters.name: Delinda Deline
characters.description: Ed's daughter, Delinda becomes the Entertainment Manager of the Montecito, overseeing the running of the casino's clubs, restaurants and shops. She has a short-lived affair with Danny at the beginning of the first season, but they later fall in love and get back together. Action Survivor: Her dad taught her how to defend herself. When a guy tries toDate Rapeher, she manages to kick his ass even while drugged. Alliterative Name:DelindaDeli'''ne. Attention Deficit... Ooh, Shiny!: She's prone to getting bored of things quickly, and moves on to the next thing that picks her interest. Bare Midriffs Are Feminine:Played for Laughswhen shecustomizes her chef uniform to expose her midriffjust because she thinks it's cute.

characters.name: Nessa Holt
characters.description: The Pit Boss of the Montecito, she oversees all betting on the floor and monitors the tables to ensure there is no cheating. Her father was a CIA agent who worked with Ed, and after his disappearance, Ed took her in and raised her like a daughter. She is of course very close to Ed, and she and Delinda consider each other sisters. Aroused by Their Voice: Danny finds her English accent and voice to be incredibly attractive. It's actually one of the first things he tells us about her. Beta Couple: She had someShip Teasewith Mike in season 2, but this got dropped anti-climatically after she left the show in theSeason Finale. Black Boss Lady: She's a black woman and the no-nonsense Pit Boss of the casino. Defrosting Ice Queen: She is explicitly described by others as an "Ice Queen", but aside from the pilot (where shemuch more stoic) she doesn't really act like one, though she still puts on a tough front with clients.

characters.name: A.J. Cooper
characters.description: A Wyoming cattle baron who buys the Montecito at the outset of season 5, little is revealed about him except that he is a former Marine and claims he got into the hotel/casino business because he wanted to learn about it. He prefers being called "Cooper" instead of "A.J.", is prone to shaking things up at the Montecito, and has a mysterious connection to Piper. Benevolent Boss: Despite his stern demeanor, he does care about his employees and often goes out of the way or looks pasts certain things for their sake. Cattle Baron: The explanation for why's he's so rich is that he's a cattle baron from Wyoming. Dropped a Bridge on Him:Averted. Im the season 5 finale, he seems to die on a plane crash, seemingly to make way for a new owner in the next season, but he's revealed to have survived at the very end of the episode. Eccentric Millionaire: He bought the Montecito casino on a whim and likes to pull big stunts just to shake things up.

characters.name: Jillian Deline
characters.description: Ed's wife and mother to Delinda. Flat Character: She doesn't get much focus compared to her family, and is basically aSatellite Characterto them. Happily Married: Played straight in the early seasons, where Ed and Jillian are a happy, loving couple, but eventually his mental decline got to her and by season 5 we learn she and Ed separated. Satellite Family Member: Whenever she shows up, her role in the plot involves either Ed or Delinda. Wet Blanket Wife: Most of her storylines involved being annoyed that Ed spends way too much time with the casino.

characters.name: Monica Mancuso
characters.description: A gold digger who inherited her husband's fortune when he passed away and uses it to buy the Montecito at the start of season 3. She quickly gives every other character a headache with her massive ego. A-Cup Angst: She is very snarky about Mary and Sam's outfits which reveal their formidable cleavage given her own slender form:Monica:(snidely)Are we opening a Hooters or something?Sam:(motioning towards Monica's chest)If we are you're in big trouble! Alliterative Name:MonicaMancuso. And There Was Much Rejoicing: This is how Monica's exit is handled. The cast is not only all-around pleased that she's gone, butthey flush her ashen remains down a toilet. Though this was according to Monica's wishes,as her will and friend explained that it was a metaphor for what Monica believed life to be like. Animal Wrongs Group: They steal a truckload of lobsters from the Montecito to prevent their "murder", and dump them into a shark aquarium, which endangers the sharks by introducing unchecked foreign contaminants into the water. Besides getting them arrested and using theRunning Gagof the threat ofPrison Rapeby suggesting he will become a meat eater, Ed and another casino manager retaliate by organizing an All-You-Can-Eat lobster buffet.

characters.name: Casey Manning
characters.description: Sam's ex-husband and one of the many owners of the Montecito. Dogged Nice Guy: He's a handsome, rich,Nice Guywho genuinely loves Sam, but she keeps blowing him off because she thinksLove Is a Weakness. Dropped a Bridge on Him: In season four, he is killed by a giant squid in a bizarre fishing accident, leaving the hotel ownership to Sam. New Old Flame: He's Sam's estranged husband, who gets involved in the casino business in order to win her back. Shout-Out: In one episodeCasey dons a pair of geeky glassesand tries to scope out the Montecito as a normal patron. Ed, who is spying on him from surveillanceimmediately calls out who would fall for that.

characters.name: Polly
characters.description: An employee of the Montecito who runs the hotel's beauty parlor. Asian Rudeness: She's rude as anything, not because she's unusually bossy or callous, but because she doesn't hesitate to prattle on and on about her sex life in mixed company. Asian Speekee Engrish: Often speaks in this type of broken English. Brutal Honesty: She tends to speak her mind on any subject and doesn't hold anything back. Bunny-Ears Lawyer: She's very eccentric, but is a legitimately good beautician.

characters.name: Mitch Sassen
characters.description: A paraplegic member of the surveillance team. Flat Character: He doesn't get much characterization other than being a bit of an oddball, and mostly exists so he can be aVoice with an Internet Connection. Genius Cripple: He's paraplegic and something of a tech genius. Most of the show'sEnhance Buttonmoments are said to work because he's good at his job. Mission Control: He often just stays at the surveillance room, relaying information to other characters (usually Danny). Voice with an Internet Connection: Characters (usually Danny) get into contact with Mitch when they want some information about something, and he'll usually provide it in no time.

characters.name: Luis Perez
characters.description: A police detective and childhood friend of Danny and Mary. Childhood Friend: He's a native of Las Vegas like Danny and Mary, and has been friends with them since they were kids. Friend on the Force: He's Danny (and by extension the Montecito's) ally in the police force, and he helps them out whenever something criminal happens at the casino. Gratuitous Spanish: Often throws in some Spanish phrase or two in his dialogue, to indicate that he's Latino. Put on a Bus to Hell: Like Danny, he gets recalled for service in Iraq. Unlike Danny, he doesn't survive.

characters.name: Kathy Berson
characters.description: The Montecito's main legal adviser and lawyer. Good Lawyers, Good Clients: She's a good woman of strong character who works for a casino that actually isn't corrupt. Iron Lady: She's a devoted and competent lawyer, who doesn't flinch when she has to tell Ed or Danny bad news, and isn't afraid to say to their face when they're being stupid. Living Lie Detector: She claims to be very good at telling when people are lying to her. Out of Focus: She mostly exists to talk about legal issues whenever the casino gets into trouble and doesn't have any personal plotlines of her own.

characters.name: Sarasvati Kumar
characters.description: An accountant for the Montecito casino. Bad Liar: By her own admission, she's very bad at lying and is uncomfortable when Ed asks her to omit things from Monica. Beta Couple: She begins a relationship with Mike in season 3. Bollywood Nerd: The only Indian employee on the casino, she's a math genius and something of a geek, though she doesn't look like one. Commonality Connection: Bonds with Mike over their geek interests.

characters.name: Erika
characters.description: A barmaid who tends the bar of the Montecito. Bartender Confidant: Worked as a bartender in a Vegas strip club, but got hired by Danny for the Montecito when she impressed him with the no-nonsense way she deals with drunks and troublemakers. Bouncer: She doubles as this when tending the bar, as she's perfectly able to deal with any troublemaker by herself. Brutal Honesty: She's always blunt, even to her bosses. Thankfully for her, Ed likes that.Ed:[after hearing she punched a client who grabbed her ass]The employers do not hit patrons here.Erika:Why not? You guys do it all the time.[Beat]Ed:[turns to Danny]Keep her. I like her. Tomboy with a Girly Streak: She acts likeThe Lad-ettebut her appearance and wardrobe is pretty girlish.

characters.name: Piper Nielsen
characters.description: A new character introduced in the fifth season, Piper is a Concierge, focusing on getting the guests whatever they want (separate from Sam, who only worries about her whales). Accidental Marriage:Mike and Piper got so drunk in one episode, they ended up getting married in one of Vegas' chapels. After some talking, they decide to stay married. Beta Couple: Has a romance with Mike in the latter half of season 5, which leads to anAccidental Marriage. Disappeared Dad: Piper grew up not knowing who her father was. It's eventually revealed that her father was a marine who was Cooper's best friend during the Vietnam War. Before he died, Cooper promised him that he would keep an eye on Piper. Fake Guest Star: She's never given a Title credit like AJ was when he joined, but despite only being regarded as a recurring character, she often times appears alongside the main cast when Season 5 promo materials appear, and most likely would have been bumped up to Main Cast status had the show not been cancelled.
